title: Counting Bits
pattern: Dynamic Programming â€” Bit Manipulation Recurrence
coreIdea: Compute the number of set bits for every number from 0 to n efficiently by reusing previously computed results, since the bit count of a number relates to the bit count of a smaller number obtained by removing its lowest set bit or right-shifting.
recognitionCues: asks for bit counts of EVERY number in a range, not just one number
hints at O(n) total instead of O(n log n) via bit-counting each number independently
approach: Build a dp array of size n+1. dp[0] = 0. For each i from 1 to n, dp[i] = dp[i >> 1] + (i & 1) â€” the bit count of i equals the bit count of i with its last bit removed (i>>1), plus 1 if the last bit is set.
